2603.23678.title: PLACID: Privacy-preserving Large language models for Acronym Clinical Inference and Disambiguation
2603.23678.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) offer transformative solutions across many domains, but healthcare integration is hindered by strict data privacy constraints. Clinical narratives are dense with ambiguous acronyms, misinterpretation these abbreviations can precipitate severe outcomes like life-threatening medication errors. While cloud-dependent LLMs excel at Acronym Disambiguation, transmitting Protected Health Information to external servers violates privacy frameworks. To bridge this gap, this study pioneers the evaluation of small-parameter models deployed entirely on-device to ensure privacy preservation. We introduce a privacy-preserving cascaded pipeline leveraging general-purpose local models to detect clinical acronyms, routing them to domain-specific biomedical models for context-relevant expansions. Results reveal that while general instruction-following models achieve high detection accuracy (~0.988), their expansion capabilities plummet (~0.655). Our cascaded approach utilizes domain-specific medical models to increase expansion accuracy to (~0.81). This novel work demonstrates that privacy-preserving, on-device (2B-10B) models deliver high-fidelity clinical acronym disambiguation support.
2603.23678.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在許多領域提供了變革性的解決方案，但醫療保健的整合受到嚴格數據隱私限制的阻礙。臨床敘述中充滿了模糊的縮寫，對這些縮寫的誤解可能導致嚴重後果，例如危及生命的用藥錯誤。雖然依賴雲端的LLMs在縮寫消歧方面表現出色，但將受保護的健康信息傳輸到外部伺服器違反了隱私框架。為了填補這一空白，本研究開創性地評估了完全在設備上部署的小參數模型，以確保隱私保護。我們引入了一個隱私保護的級聯管道，利用通用的本地模型來檢測臨床縮寫，並將其路由到特定領域的生物醫學模型進行與上下文相關的擴展。結果顯示，雖然通用的指令遵循模型在檢測準確性上達到高水平（約0.988），但其擴展能力卻驟降（約0.655）。我們的級聯方法利用特定領域的醫療模型將擴展準確性提高到（約0.81）。這項新穎的工作表明，隱私保護的在設備上（2B-10B）模型提供了高保真的臨床縮寫消歧支持。
2603.23678.author: Manjushree B. Aithal et.al.
2603.23678.authors: Manjushree B. Aithal, Ph. D., Alexander Kotz, James Mitchell, Ph. D
